arts.ttl: Chelsea one game away from Moises Caicedo nightmare as Enzo Maresca confirms huge decision
arts.sum: Given the Blues are huge favourites to beat relegation-threatened Leicester, there could be a temptation to bench him, but Maresca has confirmed that will not happen even though a booking would see him miss the London derby away at Arsenal next up. Enzo Maresca has seemingly confirmed one player that will be in his Chelsea starting XI to play Leicester City on Sunday.

arts.ttl: Chelsea put together most expensive squad in history after £1.4bn reveal
arts.sum: Boehly, speaking at the Financial Times Business of Football Summit in London last week, was quizzed on the Chelsea strategy and addressed some of the criticism that had come his way in recent months from sections of the club’s support. Ever since the acquisition of the club by a consortium led by US billionaire Todd Boehly and private equity firm Clearlake Capital, Chelsea have spent heavily in the market as part of a longer term strategy, with the club breaking the British transfer record twice in the space of 12 months with the acquisitions of Enzo Fernandez for £105million from Benfica, and Moises Caicedo for £115million from Brighton & Hove Albion.

arts.ttl: Every word Enzo Maresca said about Leicester City plight, Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall and that ‘miss you’ sign
arts.sum: Since his summer move to Stamford Bridge, Maresca and City have been on very different trajectories, Chelsea battling it out near the top of the Premier League table while the Foxes have struggled, first under Steve Cooper and now Ruud van Nistelrooy. Struggling Leicester City have a tough assignment on Sunday (2pm kick-off) when they come up against the manager who took them back to the Premier League last season.
